# VibeCody

> AI-powered developer toolchain: VibeCLI (terminal) + VibeUI (desktop editor)

VibeCody is an open-source, self-hostable AI coding assistant built in Rust and
TypeScript. It ships two products that share a common library layer:

- VibeCLI -- a terminal AI assistant with TUI, REPL, agent loop, and HTTP daemon
- VibeUI  -- a full desktop code editor built on Tauri 2 + Monaco + React

License: MIT
Repository: https://github.com/TuringWorks/vibecody
Language: Rust (backend/CLI) + TypeScript/React (frontend)
AI Providers: 23 native + OpenRouter (300+ models)
Unit Tests: 10,535 (0 failures)
UI Panels: 196+
Skill Files: 556 across 25+ industries
REPL Commands: 106+
Rust Modules: 196 (vibecli-cli/src/)
MCP Tools: 51 (file, agent, memory, security, email, calendar, tasks, Notion, Jira, Home Assistant)

## Architecture

Monorepo with 9 Cargo workspace members and a React/Vite frontend:

  vibecody/
    Cargo.toml              -- workspace root
    vibecli/vibecli-cli/    -- CLI binary (TUI + REPL + HTTP daemon)
    vibeui/                 -- Tauri desktop app (React frontend + Rust backend)
    vibeui/crates/vibe-core -- text buffer, FS, workspace, Git, terminal, search
    vibeui/crates/vibe-ai   -- 23 AI providers, agent loop, hooks, planner, tools
    vibeui/crates/vibe-lsp  -- Language Server Protocol client
    vibeui/crates/vibe-extensions -- WASM plugin runtime (Wasmtime)
    vibeui/crates/vibe-collab     -- CRDT multiplayer collaboration
    vibe-indexer/           -- remote indexing service
    vscode-extension/       -- VS Code extension
    jetbrains-plugin/       -- JetBrains IDE plugin
    neovim-plugin/          -- Neovim plugin
    packages/agent-sdk/     -- TypeScript Agent SDK

All providers implement the AIProvider trait (vibe-ai). The agent loop uses
XML-based tool calling in the system prompt, which works universally with every
provider -- no native function-calling API required.

## Providers

23 providers, listed with env var and default model:

  1. Ollama         -- (no key needed)        -- qwen3-coder:480b-cloud
  2. Claude         -- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY       -- claude-sonnet-4-6
  3. OpenAI         -- OPENAI_API_KEY          -- gpt-4o
  4. Gemini         -- GEMINI_API_KEY          -- gemini-2.5-flash
  5. Grok           -- GROK_API_KEY            -- grok-3-mini
  6. Groq           -- GROQ_API_KEY            -- llama-3.3-70b-versatile
  7. OpenRouter     -- OPENROUTER_API_KEY      -- (user-specified)
  8. Azure OpenAI   -- AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY    -- (deployment-specific)
  9. AWS Bedrock    -- AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID + AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY -- anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0
 10. GitHub Copilot -- GITHUB_TOKEN or ~/.config/github-copilot/hosts.json -- gpt-4o
 11. Mistral        -- MISTRAL_API_KEY         -- mistral-large-latest
 12. Cerebras       -- CEREBRAS_API_KEY        -- (user-specified)
 13. DeepSeek       -- DEEPSEEK_API_KEY        -- (user-specified)
 14. Zhipu          -- ZHIPU_API_KEY           -- (user-specified)
 15. Vercel AI      -- VERCEL_AI_API_KEY       -- (user-specified, requires api_url)
 16. MiniMax        -- MINIMAX_API_KEY         -- (user-specified)
 17. Perplexity     -- PERPLEXITY_API_KEY      -- (user-specified)
 18. Together       -- TOGETHER_API_KEY        -- (user-specified)
 19. Fireworks      -- FIREWORKS_API_KEY       -- (user-specified)
 20. SambaNova      -- SAMBANOVA_API_KEY       -- (user-specified)
 21. LocalEdit      -- (no key needed)         -- local edit model
 22. Failover       -- (chains other providers) -- configured via [failover] chain
 23. Custom         -- via config api_url      -- any OpenAI-compatible endpoint

## CLI Usage

  vibecli                         -- interactive REPL mode
  vibecli --tui                   -- terminal UI (Ratatui)
  vibecli --provider claude       -- select provider
  vibecli --model gpt-4o          -- select model
  vibecli --agent "fix all tests" -- non-interactive agent mode
  vibecli --exec "task" --output-format json  -- CI/exec mode
  vibecli --plan --agent "task"   -- plan before executing
  vibecli --full-auto             -- auto-approve all tool calls
  vibecli --auto-edit             -- auto-approve edits, prompt for bash
  vibecli --suggest               -- prompt before every tool call
  vibecli --serve --port 7878     -- HTTP daemon mode
  vibecli --mcp-server            -- MCP server over stdio
  vibecli --gateway telegram      -- messaging bot mode
  vibecli --review --pr 42        -- code review mode
  vibecli --doctor                -- health check
  vibecli --sandbox               -- OS-level sandbox for commands
  vibecli --worktree              -- git worktree isolation
  vibecli --voice                 -- voice input (Groq Whisper)
  vibecli --cloud                 -- run agent in Docker container
  vibecli --notebook script.vibe  -- run notebook file
  vibecli --redteam http://url    -- security scan
  vibecli --setup                 -- interactive platform setup wizard
  vibecli --service install       -- install always-on system service
  vibecli --cmd "/email unread"   -- run a single REPL command and exit
  vibecli --cmd "/cal today"      -- (email, cal, ha, todo, notion, jira, linear)
  vibecli --copilot-login         -- GitHub Copilot device auth

## Productivity Integrations

VibeCLI connects to external services via REPL commands and MCP tools:

  /email inbox|unread|read|send|search|triage|archive  -- Gmail / Outlook
  /cal today|week|list|create|delete|free|move|next    -- Google Calendar / Outlook
  /todo list|today|add|close|delete|project|search     -- Todoist task management
  /notion search|get|create|query|append               -- Notion workspace
  /jira list|create|get|comment|transition|search|mine -- Jira Cloud / Server
  /ha status|lights|on|off|toggle|set|scene|climate    -- Home Assistant smart home

Configure in ~/.vibecli/config.toml: [email], [calendar], [home_assistant], [jira]
Top-level keys: notion_api_key, todoist_api_key
Env vars: GMAIL_ACCESS_TOKEN, OUTLOOK_ACCESS_TOKEN, GOOGLE_CALENDAR_TOKEN,
          OUTLOOK_CALENDAR_TOKEN, HA_URL, HA_TOKEN, NOTION_API_KEY,
          TODOIST_API_KEY, JIRA_URL, JIRA_EMAIL, JIRA_API_TOKEN

## Deployment (12 Targets)

One-command deployment to any platform:

  Cloud:   AWS (ECS Fargate), GCP (Cloud Run), Azure (Container Apps),
           Oracle Cloud (ARM, always-free), DigitalOcean, Linode/Akamai
  Desktop: macOS (launchd service), Linux (systemd), Windows (Scheduled Task)
  Edge:    Raspberry Pi 3/4/5 (ARM, swap-optimized)

  AWS:     cd deploy/aws     && bash setup.sh [--tier lite|pro|max]
  GCP:     cd deploy/gcp     && bash setup.sh
  Azure:   cd deploy/azure   && bash setup.sh
  Oracle:  cd deploy/oracle-cloud && bash setup.sh   # always-free 4 ARM OCPU
  DO:      cd deploy/digitalocean  && bash setup.sh
  Linode:  cd deploy/linode-akamai && bash setup.sh
  macOS:   cd deploy/macos   && bash setup.sh
  Linux:   cd deploy/linux   && bash setup.sh
  Windows: deploy\windows\setup.ps1 -AlwaysOn
  Pi:      cd deploy/raspberry-pi && bash setup.sh

See docs/setup.md and docs/guides/ for full platform guides.

## Agent Tools

9 built-in tools available to the AI agent:

  read_file        -- read file contents at a path
  write_file       -- create or overwrite a file
  apply_patch      -- apply unified diff patch to a file
  bash             -- execute shell command, return stdout+stderr
  search_files     -- search files by pattern or content (ripgrep)
  list_directory   -- list files and directories at a path
  web_search       -- search the web via DuckDuckGo (no API key)
  fetch_url        -- fetch and extract text from a web page
  task_complete    -- signal task completion with summary
  spawn_agent      -- delegate sub-task to a child agent

Tool calling uses XML in the system prompt (<tool_call name="...">), which
works universally with all providers without native function-calling APIs.

## Configuration

Config file: ~/.vibecli/config.toml

Sections: [ollama], [claude], [openai], [gemini], [grok], [groq],
[openrouter], [azure_openai], [bedrock], [copilot], [mistral], [cerebras],
[deepseek], [zhipu], [vercel_ai], [minimax], [perplexity], [together],
[fireworks], [sambanova], [failover], [safety], [ui], [tools], [memory],
[routing], [gateway], [voice], [redteam], [github_app], [otel],
[sandbox_config], [email], [calendar], [home_assistant], [jira],
[[mcp_servers]], [[hooks]]

Each provider section accepts: enabled, api_key, api_url, model

## Build Commands

  make setup       -- install all prerequisites
  make cli         -- build VibeCLI release binary
  make ui          -- run VibeUI in dev mode
  make test        -- run all 9,570 tests
  make test-fast   -- run tests excluding collab crate
  make check       -- fast type-check (Rust + TypeScript)
  make lint        -- clippy + TypeScript check
  make docker      -- build Docker image
  cargo build --release -p vibecli   -- build CLI directly
  cargo test --workspace             -- all Rust tests

## Key Environment Variables

  AI providers: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, GROK_API_KEY,
    GROQ_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY,
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN,
    MISTRAL_API_KEY, CEREBRAS_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, ZHIPU_API_KEY,
    VERCEL_AI_API_KEY, MINIMAX_API_KEY, PERPLEXITY_API_KEY,
    TOGETHER_API_KEY, FIREWORKS_API_KEY, SAMBANOVA_API_KEY,
    LINEAR_API_KEY, VIBECLI_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY
  Productivity: GMAIL_ACCESS_TOKEN, OUTLOOK_ACCESS_TOKEN,
    GOOGLE_CALENDAR_TOKEN, OUTLOOK_CALENDAR_TOKEN,
    HA_URL, HA_TOKEN, NOTION_API_KEY, TODOIST_API_KEY,
    JIRA_URL, JIRA_EMAIL, JIRA_API_TOKEN

## API Endpoints (vibecli serve)

  GET  /health             -- liveness check
  POST /chat               -- single-turn chat (non-streaming)
  POST /chat/stream        -- streaming chat (SSE)
  POST /agent              -- start agent task, returns {session_id}
  GET  /stream/:session_id -- SSE stream of agent events
  GET  /jobs               -- list background jobs
  GET  /jobs/:id           -- get single job
  POST /jobs/:id/cancel    -- cancel running job
  GET  /sessions           -- HTML session index
  GET  /sessions.json      -- JSON session list
  GET  /view/:id           -- HTML session view
  GET  /share/:id          -- shareable readonly session

## Optional

- llms-full.txt: /llms-full.txt -- complete documentation for AI agents (800+ lines)
